hero.title: Subnautica 2 Wiki
hero.description: Dive into an all-new alien ocean world in solo or up to 4-player online co-op. Explore biomes, build bases, craft gear, pilot vehicles, and survive unknown creatures.

gameFeature.title: Subnautica 2 Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Explore a new alien ocean, survive deeper biome threats, build modular bases, and progress through co-op-friendly discovery systems.

lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Subnautica 2 Early Access Release Date and Platforms
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Use this section to quickly check when Subnautica 2 unlocks, where you can play it, launch pricing, and what Early Access includes.

steps.title: Early Access release date: May 14, 2026
steps.description: Subnautica 2 starts public Early Access / Game Preview rollout on May 14, 2026.

steps.title: Global launch time: 08:00 PDT / 15:00 UTC
steps.description: The global unlock window is synchronized across PC and Xbox storefront regions.

steps.title: Launch price and storefronts
steps.description: Early Access launches at $29.99 USD on Steam and Epic Games Store with regional price variation.

steps.title: Xbox and Game Pass availability
steps.description: Subnautica 2 launches on Xbox Series X|S, Xbox on PC, and Game Pass Ultimate/PC Game Pass on day one.

steps.title: Early Access roadmap and full release scope
steps.description: The current plan estimates about 2 to 3 years of Early Access with additional biomes, creatures, craftables, and story content.

steps.title: Purchase timing note
steps.description: Unknown Worlds notes the game price is planned to increase after Early Access, while Early buyers receive ongoing updates.

quickTips: Check your local timezone conversion before launch hour.
Steam and Epic are both available for PC players at launch.
Game Pass access starts immediately at Early Access launch time.
Plan for feature expansion during Early Access, not full 1.0 scope on day one.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Subnautica 2 Beginner Guide: What to Do First
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Start with safe exploration, scanning, crafting, and a compact base before pushing toward deep-biome progression.

cards.name: Stabilize basic survival
cards.description: Prioritize oxygen, water, food, and safe return routes before long-distance dives.

cards.name: Scan everything useful
cards.description: Scan lifeforms, fragments, ruins, and points of interest to expand data and unlock practical crafting targets.

cards.name: Craft before depth pushing
cards.description: Upgrade tools and mobility first, then commit to darker and deeper biome routes.

cards.name: Build a small starter base
cards.description: Use a safe-route location with storage and prep space, then expand only after supply loops stabilize.

cards.name: Use the Tadpole to expand range
cards.description: The Tadpole submersible helps extend route distance and improves deep-water logistics.

cards.name: Follow discovery cues
cards.description: Use terrain clues, scan data, beacons, and environmental signals to guide progression naturally.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Subnautica 2 Multiplayer Co-op Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Subnautica 2 supports solo play and optional online co-op for up to four players, including PC and Xbox cross-platform play.

items.name: Solo-compatible from start to finish
items.description: Subnautica 2 can be completed solo with no mandatory co-op-only progression gates.

items.name: Up to four players per world
items.description: Each co-op session supports one host plus up to three additional players.

items.name: PC and Xbox cross-play
items.description: Cross-platform multiplayer is supported between PC and Xbox ecosystems.

items.name: Host, friend invites, and friend codes
items.description: Players can host multiplayer worlds, invite friends, and use friend code flows for joining.

items.name: Drop-in and drop-out progression
items.description: Players can join active worlds and continue group progression without restarting world state.

items.name: Shared storage and shared scans
items.description: Built storage, data entries, and craftable recipe unlocks support coordinated team progression.

items.name: Collaborative or split base building
items.description: Teams can build one large base together or run separate build sites in the same world.

items.name: Vehicle sharing
items.description: Vehicles can be used by the group, and duplicate builds reduce route waiting time.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Subnautica 2 Map, Biomes, Routes, and Exploration
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Subnautica 2 shifts to a new alien ocean with layered biome progression, high-risk deep routes, scan-led discovery, and Leviathan threat zones.

solutions.name: New alien ocean world
solutions.description: Subnautica 2 takes place on a new underwater planet built around mystery-driven exploration.

solutions.name: Shallow starter routes
solutions.description: Early routes focus on safe scanning, material gathering, and reliable return planning.

solutions.name: Depth-based biome progression
solutions.description: Deeper routes open as players improve tools, vehicle upgrades, and survival stability.

solutions.name: Launch + future biome expansion
solutions.description: Early Access includes multiple biomes with additional creatures and narrative planned over time.

solutions.name: Ruins and key exploration points
solutions.description: Ruins and major scan targets drive data unlocks, route knowledge, and upgrade priorities.

solutions.name: Ocean currents as route risk
solutions.description: Currents can redirect dives into dangerous zones and require stronger return discipline.

solutions.name: Dark zones and bioluminescence
solutions.description: Lighting shifts and darker waters increase navigation challenge in deeper biomes.

solutions.name: Leviathan threat corridors
solutions.description: Large hostile fauna appear in designated danger zones and punish unprepared route entry.

managementTips: Map your oxygen-safe loop before expanding into deeper lanes.
Use scan data to sequence your biome progression and upgrades.
Treat currents as route modifiers, not background effects.
Track Leviathan sightings and avoid repeating blind approach paths.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Subnautica 2 Base Building Guide
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Subnautica 2 introduces a more procedural and sculptural base-building flow, letting you build practical starter hubs first and expand into larger personalized underwater spaces.

cards.name: Choose a safe starter location
cards.description: Build near stable resources and shallow fallback routes before committing to deeper expansion paths.

cards.name: Use procedural construction
cards.description: The new building system is less dependent on fixed prefab snapping and supports more expressive room shaping.

cards.name: Plan windows and visibility
cards.description: Place windows around intended viewpoints first, then shape connected spaces to match exploration sightlines.

cards.name: Coordinate co-op construction
cards.description: Shared lockers and joint build access make team expansion faster, whether the party uses one megabase or multiple hubs.

highlights: Keep storage and crafting near entry points
Reserve space for lighting and cosmetics
Expand modules as depth routes increase

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Subnautica 2 Vehicles and Tools
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Scan, craft, and upgrade your way through deeper biomes with shared progression systems built for both solo and co-op exploration in Subnautica 2.

regions.name: Tadpole Submersible
regions.description: The Tadpole is the core traversal vehicle for moving deeper and safer once long swims stop being practical.

regions.name: Scanner and Data Entries
regions.description: Scanning creatures, terrain, ruins, and objects expands Data Entries and reveals practical progression targets.

regions.name: Blueprint Discovery
regions.description: Finding and scanning key targets unlocks new vehicles, equipment, and craftable recipes for the whole journey.

regions.name: Craftable Tools and Upgrades
regions.description: Tool and upgrade progression supports oxygen discipline, safer harvesting, deeper scouting, and stronger return consistency.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Subnautica 2 Creatures and Leviathans
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Subnautica 2 rewards careful observation and scanning, from passive biodiversity to Leviathan-class threats that can abruptly change a route into an escape sequence.

creatures.name: Collector Leviathan
creatures.description: A reactive apex predator that responds to sound, light, vehicles, and nearby activity, demanding disciplined distance control.

creatures.name: Deepwing Brooder Leviathan
creatures.description: Officially named during the Leviathan event cycle, this creature represents one of the clearest high-risk encounter profiles.

creatures.name: Towering Leviathans
creatures.description: Large Leviathan silhouettes mark deep-zone corridors where preparation, exit routing, and calm retreat timing matter most.

creatures.name: Hostile Fauna
creatures.description: Aggressive wildlife punishes rushed movement in unfamiliar zones and should be scanned from patrol edges before close interaction.

creatures.name: Passive Small Creatures
creatures.description: Lower-risk lifeforms are valuable for Data Entries and ecosystem mapping during early and mid-depth exploration.

creatures.name: Ruins and Biome Life
creatures.description: Creature behavior near ruins and key scan zones often signals progression clues, hidden hazards, and route decisions.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Subnautica 2 System Requirements and Performance
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Review Subnautica 2 hardware targets before installation, from minimum 1080p settings to high-end 4K plans, plus handheld and console support notes.

items.name: Minimum Performance Target
items.description: GeForce GTX 1660 6 GB or Radeon RX 5500 XT 6 GB, Intel i5-8400 or Ryzen 5 2600, and 12 GB RAM for 30 FPS targets.

items.name: Recommended Performance Target
items.description: GeForce RTX 3070 8 GB or Radeon RX 6700 XT 8 GB with Intel i7-13700 or Ryzen 7 7700X and 16 GB RAM for smoother 60 FPS play.

items.name: High-End and 4K Targets
items.description: Ultra paths scale to RTX 4070-class and above, with 4K high settings centered around RTX 5070 Ti or Radeon RX 7900 XTX-level hardware.

items.name: Platform and API Requirements
items.description: Windows 10/11 64-bit and DirectX 12 are required, with 50 GB storage and broadband connectivity listed across targets.

items.name: Handheld and Console Notes
items.description: Steam Deck is verified, while Xbox Series platforms and the ROG Xbox Ally series are confirmed launch-ready options.

items.name: Early Access Optimization Outlook
items.description: Optimization and hardware tuning are expected to improve throughout Early Access as additional content and systems roll out.

unlockMilestones: Match GPU class to expected preset

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Subnautica 2 Farming and Growth
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Growth is part of real progression in Subnautica 2. Farming is not only about planting and harvesting, but also about setting up repeatable material routes that keep your tools, weapons, and crafting options flowing.

sections.name: Seed Progression
sections.description: Planting and fertilizing seeds is a real milestone in the game, and harvesting a fully-grown nut is part of the same growth path.

sections.name: Material Farming
sections.description: A practical early farming loop is creating Mercury Capsules, which spawn small slimes that drop Tendrils and goo for repeatable crafting materials.

sections.name: Tool Sustain
sections.description: Resource farming becomes much stronger once you can consistently gather Osmium, glass shards, plants, and slime materials for follow-up crafts.

sections.name: Base Routine
sections.description: The best farming setup supports more than food. It should feed your storage, movement tools, weapon upgrades, and future fusion experiments.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Subnautica 2 Best Early Unlocks
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: The strongest early unlocks are the ones that improve safety, storage, and movement. In Subnautica 2, a few practical upgrades can make the entire rest of the game much smoother.

priorities.name: Personal Qualia
priorities.description: As soon as you get access to your personal Qualia, your run becomes much more stable. It acts as a reliable hub for storage, building, and regrouping.

priorities.name: Frost Pick
priorities.description: A fast and cheap early mining tool made from Osmium and Stick. It is one of the most useful upgrades for accelerating early resource collection.

priorities.name: Cache Cube
priorities.description: Storage pressure starts early, so getting your first dependable chest-style container is a major quality-of-life upgrade.

priorities.name: Hookshot
priorities.description: The hookshot is one of the clearest movement milestones in the game and quickly makes exploration faster and safer.

priorities.name: Glider Glove
priorities.description: The glider glove adds another major mobility boost and makes long-distance movement far more efficient.

priorities.name: Projectile Wand or Bombs
priorities.description: A ranged tool or explosive option gives you a safer answer to dangerous enemies and trial-style encounters.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Subnautica 2 Achievement Tracker
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Subnautica 2 currently has 19 Steam achievements, and they naturally split into exploration, crafting, mobility, combat, and growth progression.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Subnautica 2 Singleplayer and Platform FAQ
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: These are the core platform and access questions players keep asking before buying Subnautica 2.

faqs.question: Is Subnautica 2 singleplayer?
faqs.answer: Yes. Subnautica 2 is a singleplayer game.

faqs.question: Is Subnautica 2 Subnautica 2 multiplayer or co-op planned?
faqs.answer: No. Multiplayer is not planned for release or future updates.

faqs.question: Where can I play Subnautica 2?
faqs.answer: It is currently available on Steam for Windows.

faqs.question: Does Subnautica 2 support controllers?
faqs.answer: Yes, but controller play is not recommended because the menus are not designed around it.

faqs.question: Does Subnautica 2 work on Steam Deck?
faqs.answer: Yes. Proton 9.0 or 9.0.4 is the commonly recommended setup for Steam Deck.

faqs.question: Does Subnautica 2 work on Linux?
faqs.answer: Yes. Proton 9.0 is reported to work well for Linux and Steam Deck.

faqs.question: Will Subnautica 2 Subnautica 2 Mac or Android be supported?
faqs.answer: They may be supported in the future, but they are not current launch platforms.

faqs.question: Does Subnautica 2 have translations?
faqs.answer: No. There are currently no translations planned.

faqs.question: Will Subnautica 2 keep getting updates?
faqs.answer: Yes. Some content updates are planned after release, but they are not intended to continue on an indefinite regular schedule.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Subnautica 2 Steam Deck and Controller
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Subnautica 2 can run on Steam Deck, but the default setup may need adjustment. Keyboard and mouse remains the smoother option for menus and general play.

faqs.question: Does Subnautica 2 work on Steam Deck?
faqs.answer: Yes. Proton 9.0 works well, and Proton 9.0.4 is a commonly shared fix for Steam Deck issues.

faqs.question: What Subnautica 2 in Subnautica 2 should I do if Steam Deck controls are not working?
faqs.answer: Force compatibility and switch the game to Proton 9.0.4. The default configuration may not accept controller input correctly.

faqs.question: Does Subnautica 2 support controllers?
faqs.answer: Yes, but controller play is not recommended because the game's menus are not designed around it.

faqs.question: Is Subnautica 2 Subnautica 2 Linux support possible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Proton 9.0 is reported to work well for Linux and Steam Deck.

faqs.question: What Subnautica 2 in Subnautica 2 is the best input setup?
faqs.answer: Keyboard and mouse is the smoothest option for menu navigation and general moment-to-moment play.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Subnautica 2 Settings and Accessibility
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Subnautica 2 already has a few practical settings and compatibility notes that matter immediately when players first launch the game.

settings.name: Display Settings
settings.description: Display Settings includes an option that lets players hide the on-screen hands.

settings.name: Graphics Compatibility
settings.description: Vulkan support is required for the graphics to render properly. Systems without Vulkan can run into crashes and severe graphical glitches.

settings.name: Input Preference
settings.description: Controller support exists, but keyboard and mouse gives a smoother experience because menus are not built around controller use.

settings.name: Language Support
settings.description: The current setup is English-only, and no translations are planned right now.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Subnautica 2 Updates and Patch Notes
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Subnautica 2 is already receiving quick post-launch fixes and small usability updates.

entries.title: Update 2.8.2
entries.description: Renamed "awaken" to "exit" in the main menu.

entries.title: Small Patch for Start-Up Crashes
entries.description: A dedicated patch was released for players whose game was crashing on launch.

entries.title: Post-Launch Content Support
entries.description: Some content updates are planned after release, but they are not intended to continue on an indefinite regular schedule.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Subnautica 2 Crash Fix and Troubleshooting
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Most immediate launch and crash issues should be checked in a simple order: patch level, graphics support, platform compatibility, and logs.

steps.title: Install the Latest Crash Hotfix
steps.description: If the game is crashing on start, update first and test again with the latest start-up crash patch.

steps.title: Check Vulkan Support
steps.description: Subnautica 2 requires Vulkan support. Systems without Vulkan can suffer from crashes or major rendering problems.

steps.title: Use Proton 9.0.4 on Steam Deck
steps.description: If Steam Deck input or launch behavior is broken, force compatibility and switch to Proton 9.0.4.

steps.title: Collect Crash Logs
steps.description: Open Windows Run, paste %APPDATA%, go to the game logs folder, and gather the .txt files for troubleshooting.

steps.title: Report the Issue Properly
steps.description: Share the logs and device details through the official Discord bug-reports forum or on the Steam community page.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before diving into Subnautica 2

questions.question: Is Subnautica 2 a singleplayer game?
questions.answer: Yes. Subnautica 2 is a singleplayer-only game with no multiplayer or co-op planned.

questions.question: What is the Apotheosis system in Subnautica 2?
questions.answer: Apotheosis is Subnautica 2' freeform fusion crafting system. Instead of following fixed recipes, you combine materials and discover your own crafting paths.

questions.question: Where can I play Subnautica 2?
questions.answer: Subnautica 2 is currently available on Steam for Windows. Linux and Steam Deck support is available through Proton 9.0.

questions.question: Does Subnautica 2 support controllers?
questions.answer: Yes, but controller play is not recommended because the menus are not designed around it. Keyboard and mouse is the smoother option.

questions.question: Will Subnautica 2 receive updates?
questions.answer: Yes. Some content updates are planned after release, but they are not intended to continue on an indefinite regular schedule.
